items.title: M Resources, Jindal Steel two remaining Whyalla steelworks bidders, while BlueScope still retains last offer right

children.content: The federal and state governments have reduced the number of bidders for the financially stricken Whyalla steelworks from five to two, but BlueScope could still purchase it if they make a better offer.

gallery.altText: Workers at the Whyalla steelworks blast furnace.

thumbnail.altText: Workers at the Whyalla steelworks blast furnace.

items.title: The extraordinary legacy of a trailblazing female driving instructor

children.content: One of South Australia's first female licensed driving instructors has been remembered for her tireless advocacy and devotion to her community.

image.altText: Black and white photo from newspaper showing woman speaking into a microphone

items.title: Murderer convicted of 1999 crime back in custody for breaching parole

children.content: Murderer Garry Thompson has been returned to custody for 12 months for breaching his parole conditions multiple times.

image.altText: The exterior of the Supreme Court of South Australia.

items.title: Outback school finally set to be rebuilt after mould forced demolition

children.content: More than two years since a health hazard led to Broken Hill's Willyama High School being knocked down, work on the "world-class" facility planned to replace is expected to begin.

image.altText: Five people in high-vis vests and hard hats with shovels of dirt at a construction site.

items.title: Police officer accused of rape denied he was 'sleazy', court told

children.content: A police officer goes on trial in South Australia's District Court after pleading not guilty to two counts of rape and one count of attempted rape.

image.altText: An old stone building with a red dome

items.title: Rain brings potential 'opportunities' in renewed search for Gus Lamont, police say

children.content: Eight months since Gus Lamont was reported missing, police have again returned to search Oak Park Station as part of an ongoing investigations into the four-year-old's disappearance.

articles.title: SA Police return to Yunta as part of Gus Lamont investigation

children.content: Police have returned to Yunta in South Australia's far north as part of their investigation into the disappearance of four-year-old boy Gus Lamont.

image.altText: Gus Lamont

articles.title: Where is Gus? Six months on, more questions than answers remain

children.content: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

image.altText: Composite image of missing boy Gus Lamont imposed on an image of the vast outback with a group of police.

items.title: SAPOL commissioner rejects outback police shortage plan

children.content: Commissioner Grant Stevens rejects recommendations to attract more officers to the understaffed Coober Pedy station over funding concerns.

image.altText: An aerial view of the township of Coober Pedy

items.title: Young farmer who died two weeks after engagement set to leave lasting legacy

children.content: A scholarship will be created at a Victorian agricultural college in memory of a young man who died in a farming accident last month.

image.altText: Man and woman wearing cowboy hats, sitting on a log

items.title: Archibald Prize sitter calls out APY art collective mistreatment

children.content: Award-winning artist Iluwanti Ken says the withdrawal of funding and support over the "white hands on black art" scandal has left her feeling like a stranger in her own industry.

image.altText: A seated Aboriginal woman holds a microphone and gestures with her free hand.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: Late pastoralist's family loses appeal over judgement that father was responsible for bushfire

children.content: A court judgement that found pastoralist Tom Brinkworth liable for a 2021 bushfire, five months after he died, has been upheld after an appeal by Mr Brinkworth's family.

image.altText: Smoke about the Lucindale grass fire

items.title: 'A devastating mess': Owner laments destruction and theft of organ pipes at former church

children.content: The pipe theft comes days after a bronze statue was stolen from the centre of a fountain in Adelaide's inner north-eastern suburbs.

gallery.altText: A man standing in a darkened church with organ keys in the foreground

thumbnail.altText: A man standing in a darkened church with organ keys in the foreground

items.title: Driver dies in head-on crash with road train that forced Stuart Highway closure

children.content: A man from the Northern Territory has been killed in a head-on crash with a truck that forced the closure of the Stuart Highway in South Australia's far north earlier today.

image.altText: Road signage on the side of a highway intersection in the Northern Territory.

items.title: Flights resume at training school after two killed in plane crash

children.content: An aviation school resumes flight operations at Adelaide's Parafield Airport four weeks after an instructor and a student onboard a twin-engine plane were killed when it crashed into a hangar.

image.altText: Black smoke billows from a plane crash at Parafield Airport

items.title: Cricket's next generation of pace knows fast bowling can take a toll

children.content: Cricket has few more glorious sights than a fast bowler terrorising a top-class batting order, but becoming a speedster is not without its pains, as the nation's next generation is finding out.

image.altText: Simrth Gill tosses up a cricket ball, while holding a cricket bat.

items.title: The BHP files reveal a big problem facing the mining giant

children.content: For a company that prides itself on digging out the metals for the world's clean energy future, saying nothing about its own clean energy future is not an option.

image.altText: Mining infrastructure and machinery in pits of red dirt.

items.title: Why drones fell from the sky during Sydney's Vivid light show

children.content: The company behind the drone shows at Sydney's Vivid festival said spectators were not at risk when almost 90 unmanned aircraft fell from the sky during a display on Monday night.

image.altText: Screengrab from mobile phone video shows purple drones in ragged formation over body of water with city in background at night

items.title: Iran calls latest US strikes 'bad faith' sign as internet slowly restored

children.content: Iran has denounced US strikes in the country's south as a sign of "bad faith and unreliability" as negotiations toward a possible deal to end the war continue.

image.altText: Smoke billows across a city.

items.title: Australian students record worst tech literacy results on record

children.content: Australian students have recorded their worst-ever results in national tests that measure digital literacy, with just 37 per cent of year 10 students and 50 per cent of year 6 students assessed as proficient.

image.altText: A pair of unidentifiable hands type on a laptop, with other people on laptops in the background.

items.title: Koalas at risk of death once seven-day temps rise beyond 27C

children.content: The risks of death or hospitalisation of koalas increases as soon as seven-day average maximum temperature is above 27 degrees Celsius, a new study shows.

gallery.altText: Landscape shot of a gap in two trees with a koala head in between with its eyes closed under harsh sunlight.

thumbnail.altText: Landscape shot of a gap in two trees with a koala head in between with its eyes closed under harsh sunlight.

items.title: Women and children linked to ISIS spend first night back in Australia

children.content: A group of so-called ISIS brides who left the notorious al-Roj camp in Syria last week have spent their first night home in Australia.

image.altText: A woman in back seat of a car wearing a face mask.

items.title: Two men released without charge after Dezi Freeman-related arrest

children.content: A 48-year-old and a 45-year-old were taken into custody by detectives from Taskforce Summit, who continued to investigate Freeman's movements after the fatal shooting of officers Neal Thompson and Vadim de Waart-Hottart at a Porepunkah property in Victoria's north-east in August last year.

image.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: 'Not far away': Rescuers confident of reaching group trapped in Laos cave

children.content: Seven people remain trapped after heavy rain triggered a landslide but rescuers say they are close to reaching the group.

image.altText: Four men stand at a narrow cave entrance wearing helmets and carrying ropes.

items.title: Why Blak Douglas won't enter the Archibald Prize again

children.content: The acclaimed Dunghutti artist is using his latest exhibition to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art and the systemic issues creatives face in Australia.

gallery.altText: Artist Blak Douglas, born Adam Douglas Hill, has opened a new exhibition at NSW Parliament House entitled 'Home Flown'.

thumbnail.altText: Artist Blak Douglas, born Adam Douglas Hill, has opened a new exhibition at NSW Parliament House entitled 'Home Flown'.

items.title: Next steps to take after receiving life-altering ancestry DNA test results

children.content: Direct-to-consumer genetic testing kits are often given as gifts or purchased "as a bit of fun", which means people often aren't prepared to discover unexpected parents, siblings and other relatives.

image.altText: man looking at phone with shocked expression

items.title: More or Less quiz: We need you to rank five State of Origin greats

children.content: If you think you know your stuff when it comes to State of Origin, here's your chance to prove it.

items.title: 'Did you get any of it right?': Senator calls out handling of $15m court contract

children.content: Litigants in at least 146 court matters were potentially involved in a data breach that is now the subject of a formal complaint with the privacy commissioner in Canada, Senate estimates has been told.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and glasses and maroon tie speaking into microphone.

items.title: 'None of this was forecast': Energy demand from AI data centres soars

children.content: A new report concludes the AI-fuelled surge of power-hungry data centres across Australia is jeopardising the country's energy transition.

items.title: BP removes chair over 'serious concerns' about conduct

children.content: The BBC reported Albert Manifold's departure followed concerns about "bullying" and "overbearing" behaviour, which he later described as a "false narrative".

image.altText: A man smiling in a suit.

items.title: Student says WA school closed by toxic baits had been overrun by mice

children.content: A year 12 student at the WA College of Agriculture Morawa says there were fears of "cooking" mice in the heating system before the mistaken deployment of toxic baits shut the school down.

image.altText: A sign for The Western Australian College of Agriculture Morawa campus in wood and gold metal

items.title: Town sites closed for testing after kids find liquid mercury vials at tip

children.content: A south-west Queensland town's school, service station, hospital waiting room and several homes are closed for testing after children found liquid mercury vials at the tip and took them to school.

image.altText: Two fire officers put on hazmat suits with tank and masks with the help of colleagues.

items.title: 'Can't put it into words': Origin star's mum reveals heartbreaking call

children.content: Cameron Munster's mum reveals the heartbreaking call she had to make before her superstar son led Queensland to victory days after the shock death of his dad.

articles.title: 'Kidding ourselves': Daley whacks talk of Cleary's losing Origin record

children.content: NSW coach Laurie Daley says the responsibility does not solely rest on the shoulders of his star No.7 ahead of this year's State of Origin campaign.

image.altText: Nathan Cleary stands with his hands on his hips

articles.title: The NSW Blues made a 'tough' selection call, so will the Wallabies

children.content: Just as NSW Blues coach Laurie Daley had a difficult selection decision to make, Joe Schmidt may be thinking long and hard about one position in the Wallabies' back line for July's Nations Championship Tests.

items.title: Birrell stuns fifth seed as Osaka and Sabalenka dazzle at French Open

children.content: Australia's Kimberly Birrell continues a day of upsets for Australia by advancing to the second round of the French Open for the first time in her career.

items.title: Australian underdog stuns former world number one at French Open

children.content: Queenslander Adam Walton makes it through the heat and five-set grind to down former world number one Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.
